questions.html: Which creates a list with the items Michael, Ryan, Henry?
questions.notice: names = ["Michael", "Ryan", "Henry"] — use square brackets and separate items with commas

questions.html: Which creates a list with the numbers 1, 2, 3?
questions.notice: numbers = [1, 2, 3] — lists use square brackets, not round or curly

questions.html: Which creates a list with the colours red, blue, green?
questions.notice: colours = ["red", "blue", "green"] — strings need quotes and items need commas between them

questions.html: Which creates a list with the items cat, dog, fish?
questions.notice: pets = ["cat", "dog", "fish"] — don't forget the square brackets around the whole list

questions.html: Which creates a list with the scores 10, 20, 30?
questions.notice: scores = [10, 20, 30] — every item in the list needs a comma after it except the last

questions.html: Which creates a list with the cities London, Paris, Rome?
questions.notice: cities = ["London", "Paris", "Rome"] — city names are strings so they need quote marks

questions.html: Which creates a list with the items True, False, True?
questions.notice: flags = [True, False, True] — Python booleans are capitalised: True and False

questions.html: Which creates a list with the fruits apple, banana, cherry?
questions.notice: fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"] — make sure the closing square bracket is there

questions.html: Which creates a list with the ages 15, 22, 37?
questions.notice: ages = [15, 22, 37] — check every number matches exactly and commas are in place

questions.html: Which creates a list with the planets Mars, Venus, Earth?
questions.notice: planets = ["Mars", "Venus", "Earth"] — planet names are strings and need quote marks

questions.html: Which creates a list with the items 5, 10, 15, 20?
questions.notice: evens = [5, 10, 15, 20] — four items, four commas between them, square brackets

questions.html: Which creates a list with the names Alice, Bob?
questions.notice: players = ["Alice", "Bob"] — even with just two items you still need a comma between them
items = [] — an empty list is just two square brackets with nothing inside

questions.html: Which creates a list with the days Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday?
questions.notice: days = ["Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday"] — day names are strings so they need quote marks

questions.html: Which creates a list with the values 0.5, 1.5, 2.5?
questions.notice: decimals = [0.5, 1.5, 2.5] — decimal numbers don't need quotes, just commas between them
